[[Logan Tomkins]] (brother) }} '''Sam Tomkins''' (born 23 March 1989) is a professional [[rugby league]] analyst for [[Sky Sports]] and former professional player, having finished his playing career with [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]]. He is also the Team Manager of the [[England national rugby league team]], working in a "behind the scenes role" for head coach [[Shaun Wane]].{{Cite web |date=2024-09-04 |title=England v Samoa: Sam Tomkins named England team manager |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/articles/cly97zejw2do |access-date=2024-09-05 |website=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}} Tomkins, a product of the [[Wigan Warriors]] academy, played as a {{rlp|fb}} and occasional {{rlp|so}}, across two stints for the Warriors as well as his time at Catalans Dragons. In addition, he also played two seasons in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] for [[New Zealand Warriors]]. He is the two time [[Man of Steel Awards|Man of Steel]], earning the title in 2012 and 2021. He won three Super League Grand Finals with the [[Wigan Warriors]] in [[2010 Super League Grand Final|2010]], [[2013 Super League Grand Final|2013]], and [[2018 Super League Grand Final|2018]], as well as two Challenge Cup finals in [[2011 Challenge Cup|2011]] and [[2013 Challenge Cup|2013]]. == Early life == Tomkins was born on 23 March 1989 in [[Milton Keynes]], Buckinghamshire, England.{{cite web|url=http://englandrl.co.uk/squads/england-men/57|title=Sam Tomkins|publisher=England Rugby League|access-date=2 July 2011|archive-date=15 August 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110815174304/http://englandrl.co.uk/squads/england-men/57|url-status=live}} Following the birth of Sam's older brother [[Joel Tomkins|Joel]] in [[Warrington]], the family had moved to Milton Keynes due to his father's work.{{cite news|last=Laybourn|first=Ian|title=Milton Keynes makes its mark|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/09/09/15/RUGBYL_Column.html|access-date=2 July 2011|newspaper=Sporting Life}} In the early 1990s, his family returned to the [[North West England |North West]], to [[Chorley]], where his younger brother Logan was born. Tomkins' first experience of rugby league came when he was seven years old, after his father introduced both Sam and Joel to the Chorley Panthers ARLFC.{{cite web|last=Skoyles|first=Chris|title=Interview with Sam Tomkins|url=http://www.wlct.org.uk/culture/linconline/samtomkins.htm|work=Wigan Leisure & Culture Trust|access-date=2 July 2011}}{{dead link|date=February 2018|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}} By the time Tomkins was nine, both he and his older brother had moved from the Chorley Panthers and were becoming more involved with the amateur [[Wigan St Patricks|Wigan St. Patrick]]'s club, a [[Farm team|feeder team]] for the professional [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] club.{{cite news|last=Christopher|first=Irvine|title=Sam Tomkins ready to run Australia gauntlet|url=http://www.timesonline.co.uk/tol/sport/rugby_league/article6897452.ece|access-date=2 July 2011|newspaper=The Times|date=31 October 2009|archive-date=7 November 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211107232436/https://www.thetimes.co.uk/|url-status=dead}} To aid their careers, the family decided to move the short distance from Chorley to [[Wigan]], and Tomkins remained playing at the club until his mid-teens. Reflecting on Tomkins' time at Wigan St. Patrick's, Bill Atherton, the club's chairman, remarked that "you could see there was something special when Sam came up through the club. There has always been something different about him." When he was aged twelve, he obtained a place on the Wigan Warriors scholarship scheme, and when he turned sixteen, Tomkins was offered a part-time contract for Wigan Warriors Youth Development club's academy. By 2007, Tomkins was featuring regularly for the Wigan academy sides. That year, he featured in an under-18s win for the [[England national rugby league team|England Academy]] against [[France national rugby league team|France Cadets]]; his early try helped establish an early England lead before he completed his brace in the second half after a sin-binning for a high tackle.{{cite web|title=France Cadets 12 England Academy Under 18's 40|url=http://www.therfl.co.uk/news/article/8188|work=Rugby Football League|access-date=2 July 2011|date=11 July 2007|archive-date=21 September 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120921020425/http://www.therfl.co.uk/news/article/8188|url-status=live}} At the end of the year, Wigan rewarded Tomkins with a full-time contract, giving him the opportunity to train alongside players in the club's first team.{{cite news|last=Chronnell|first=Liam|title=Warriors invest in their future|url=http://www.theboltonnews.co.uk/sport/1877972.warriors_invest_in_their_future|access-date=2 July 2011|newspaper=The Bolton News|date=3 December 2007|archive-date=4 October 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131004234820/http://www.theboltonnews.co.uk/sport/1877972.warriors_invest_in_their_future/|url-status=live}} == Club career == ===Wigan Warriors=== ====Debut season==== When Wigan agreed to release {{rlp|fe}} [[Trent Barrett]] from his contract, the club looked to find a long-term replacement. Tomkins was offered a one-year extension to his own contract, which would keep him at the club until the end of the 2010 season with another year optional.{{cn|date=June 2020}} After signing the extension, Wigan head coach [[Brian Noble (rugby league)|Brian Noble]] commented that Tomkins, along with Wigan teammate [[Mark Flanagan (rugby league)|Mark Flanagan]], "both have the ability to make an impact in Super League in years to come."{{cite news|last=Hadfield|first=Dave|title=Rovers revel in Dobson's choice as Wigan miss out|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/rovers-revel-in-dobsons-choice-as-wigan-miss-out-822663.html|access-date=1 July 2011|newspaper=The Independent|date=8 May 2008|archive-date=11 November 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121111161256/http://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/rovers-revel-in-dobsons-choice-as-wigan-miss-out-822663.html|url-status=live}} {{Quote box |quote = Trent is one of the world's best players at the moment. Just to play with him is an honour. You pick up so much off him and he's just a great player to play with. Joel was so supportive when I found out I was playing and really tipped me up on what to do – don't try and do too much, nothing silly. He's been a big help. |source = Sam Tomkins, 14 May 2008, commenting on the influence of Trent Barrett and his brother Joel on his début performance against Whitehaven.{{cite news|title=Warriors sign [[File:Sam Tomkins.JPG|right|thumb|Sam Tomkins playing against St Helens in 2009]] In the same year he made his league début, Tomkins was named in the [[Super League Dream Team]] at stand-off, and was also voted by journalists in the [[Rugby League Writers' Association]] as Super League Young Player of the Year.{{cite web|title=Tomkins in Super League dream team|url=http://superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=15321|work=Super League|publisher=Rugby Football League|access-date=30 June 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110830064636/http://superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=15321|archive-date=30 August 2011|url-status=dead}}{{cite web|title=Giants show their strength|url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=15577|work=Super League|publisher=Rugby Football League|access-date=30 June 2011|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120910173620/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article.php?id=15577|archive-date=10 September 2012}} At the Wigan's own awards evening, he was named Player of the Year and Young Player of the Year.{{cite web|title=Tomkins Wins Player of the Year|url=http://www.wiganwarriors.com/WContent.aspx?id=4157&type=news|work=Wigan Warriors|access-date=30 June 2011|date=22 September 2009|archive-date=25 March 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120325093136/http://www.wiganwarriors.com/WContent.aspx?id=4157&type=news|url-status=live}} Tomkins would go on to play for England during the [[2009 Rugby League Four Nations|2009 Four Nations]]. 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191107132322/https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/05/warrington-wigan-super-league-grand-final|url-status=live}} === New Zealand Warriors=== Tomkins' first games for the Warriors were in the inaugural [[Auckland Nines]] tournament held during the 2014 NRL pre-season. His first official game came in the first round of the new NRL season against the [[Parramatta Eels]]. The Warriors lost 36–16. In his second game, against the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]], he scored a try in the Warriors' 31–12 loss. He scored his second try of the season in the Warriors' 42-18 thumping against the [[Wests Tigers|Tigers]] in round 4 of the season. [[File:066tomkinzoom.jpg|thumb|300px|Warriors fullback Sam Tomkins during a game against the Sydney Roosters in 2014]] He had his breakout game for the New Zealand club in the round 18 48–0 victory over the [[Parramatta Eels]]. Tomkins scored two tries, one of which was directly from the lock position after a scrum win against the feed. The other of which, also from a scrum play, was down the short side where he broke four tackles in a fantastic individual effort. He also set up a brilliant try for captain [[Simon Mannering]]. In the first half, Tomkins recorded nine tackle breaks, a try assist, two line breaks, 84 running metres and two tries.{{Cite web |url=http://live.nrlstats.com/matches/nrl/match33193.html |title=Warriors v Parramatta, Full Time 48-0 - NRL Results - NRL Stats |access-date=16 July 2014 |archive-date=25 July 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140725193958/http://live.nrlstats.com/matches/nrl/match33193.html |url-status=live }} His first half performance truly put him on the map in the [[National Rugby League]] after some debate previous to this game over whether he was worth the big money. Tomkins presence at the Warriors saw long term club and [[New Zealand national rugby league team]] fullback [[Kevin Locke (rugby league)|Kevin Locke]] out of favour and languishing in the [[NSW Cup]] side. As a consequence of Tomkins good form, Locke was released by the club mid-season so he could join English side [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]]. [[File:Sam Tomkins New Zealand Warriors.jpg|thumb|right|Tomkins representing the New Zealand Warriors in 2015]] The Warriors announced that Tomkins would leave the club at the end of 2015 due to injury and suffering from homesickness.{{cite web| url=http://www.warriors.co.nz/news/2015/04/08/club_makes_double_fu.html| title=Club makes double fullback announcement| publisher=warriors.co.nz| date=8 April 2015| access-date=8 April 2015| archive-date=10 April 2015| archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150410213436/http://www.warriors.co.nz/news/2015/04/08/club_makes_double_fu.html| url-status=live}} === Return to Wigan === ==== 2016 ==== Tomkins returned to Wigan after two seasons in the NRL with [[New Zealand Warriors]] for £200,000 signing a four-year contract as their marquee player.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/apr/16/sam-tomkins-wigan-return-new-zealand|title=Sam Tomkins to return to Wigan Warriors and Super League next season|last=Bower|first=Aaron|date=2015-04-16|website=The Guardian|access-date=2018-01-16|archive-date=17 January 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180117132053/https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/apr/16/sam-tomkins-wigan-return-new-zealand|url-status=live}} He underwent surgery on a posterior cruciate ligament injury in November 2015 ruling him out for the start of the 2016 [[Super League]] season, however due to complications he did not appear for Wigan until 13 May at home against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]]. He went on to make 16 appearances scoring 7 tries before undergoing more knee surgery meaning he missed the start of the 2017 season. ==== 2017 ==== Tomkins did not play for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] until the [[Challenge Cup]] quarter final against the [[Warrington Wolves]] where it was his drop goal that won the tie for Wigan.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/jun/17/warrington-wolves-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup-quarter-final-match-report|title=Sam Tomkins's drop goal sends Wigan past Warrington in Challenge Cup|last=Bower|first=Aaron|date=2017-06-17|website=The Guardian|access-date=2018-01-16|archive-date=18 January 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180118010544/https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/jun/17/warrington-wolves-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup-quarter-final-match-report|url-status=live}} He quickly settled into the squad with two assists in his first two [[Super League]] games of the year before scoring a try against [[Catalans Dragons]] in Round 21. He continued his good form with tries against [[Leeds Rhinos]] and [[Huddersfield Giants]]. He played in the [[2017 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|work=Mirror|date=26 August 2017|access-date=6 November 2019|archive-date=6 November 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191106125620/https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|url-status=live}} His fifth and last try of the season came against [[Castleford Tigers]] in the 6th Round of the Super 8's. ==== 2018 ==== After struggling with knee injuries for the previous two seasons, Tomkins was available to play in the [[Super League]] from Round 1 against [[Salford Red Devils]]. His only contribution in the first three games was an assist in the first game however he showed that he may be back to his best after a 65m run to score a try against the [[Widnes Vikings]] in Round 4. After catching a [[Tom Gilmore (rugby league)|Tom Gilmore]] kick on the full he beat three defenders to race away to score his first try of the season. In Round 5 he got four assists showing that he was back to his best in the fullback role but thriving when rotated into halfback when [[Morgan Escaré]] came off the bench. Four assists and two tries followed in the next three games before a 45m drop goal won the game against [[Leeds Rhinos]] in a tense 8-9 thriller. Two tries against Round 14 opponents [[Salford Red Devils]] and one against [[Challenge Cup]] opponents [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] preceded the news that he would be leaving to join the [[Catalans Dragons]] in 2019 which put an end to months of speculation.{{Cite web|url=https://www.wiganwarriors.com/news/2018-05-14-tomkins-to-leave-warriors|title=Tomkins to leave Warriors {{!}} News {{!}} Wigan Warriors|website=www.wiganwarriors.com|access-date=2018-09-21|archive-date=21 September 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180921230142/https://www.wiganwarriors.com/news/2018-05-14-tomkins-to-leave-warriors|url-status=live}} Further tries against [[Warrington Wolves]] at the [[Magic Weekend|Dacia Magic Weekend]] in Newcastle, [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Huddersfield Giants]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] and future employers [[Catalans Dragons]] pushed his tally to 11 for the season. As the primary kicker in the [[Wigan Warriors]] side he scored 95 goals from 127 attempts giving him an accuracy level of 75%, this along a relatively injury free year helped propel [[Wigan Warriors]] to second on the [[Super League]] table ensuring a home semi final against either the [[Castleford Tigers]] or the [[Warrington Wolves]]. The year was not without event however as in early June a video of Sam and brother [[Joel Tomkins|Joel]] emerged appearing to show the two being abusive towards bar staff at a local pub. For his part in the incident Sam was fined £5,000 however Joel who was the main culprit in the abusive rant was fined £10,000 and suspended before later being released.{{Cite |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }} On 5 October 2021, Tomkins was awarded with the [[Man of Steel Awards|Man of Steel]] for his efforts with Catalans throughout the season. On 9 October 2021, Tomkins played for Catalans in their [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] loss against St. Helens. In April 2022, Tomkins fractured his [[fibula]] in the [[2022 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]] quarterfinal against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] ruling him out for a minimum of four weeks.{{cite web | url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/2643/tomkins-to-miss-inaugural-french-derby | title=Tomkins to miss inaugural French derby }} In round 20, Tomkins kicked a drop goal to win the game for Catalans against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] 13-12.{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|work=BBC Sport |date=9 October 2021 |access-date=18 October 2021|archive-date=16 October 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211016182230/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|url-status=live}} Tomkins played 21 games for Catalans in the 2022 Super League season including their elimination playoff loss to [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62813693|title=Super League play-offs: Catalans Dragons 10-20 Leeds Rhinos - Liam Sutcliffe hat-trick secures win|work=BBC Sport |date=7 September 2022 }} In March 2023, Tomkins announced that he would be retiring at the end of the season, following issues with his knee after injuring it in 2022.{{cite web |title='I can't do it much longer': Sam Tomkins to retire at end of Super League season |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/mar/10/i-cant-do-it-much-longer-sam-tomkins-to-retire-at-end-of-super-league-season |website=The Guardian |access-date=9 September 2023 |date=10 March 2023}} He delayed his decision to retire in order to play in the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup|2021 World Cup]].{{cite web | url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/sam-tomkins-world-cup-dream-delayed-retirement-decision | title=Sam Tomkins: World Cup dream delayed retirement decision | date=4 April 2023 }} In the 2023 semi-final against St Helens, Tomkins scored the winning try with less than one minute remaining to take Catalans into the grand final and also end St Helens four-year dominance of the competition. On 14 October, Tomkins played in Catalans [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] which was also his final game as a player.{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|newspaper=The Observer|date=14 October 2023 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }} ====Return==== In July 2024, Tomkins announced a return to Catalans Dragons, coming out of retirement until the end of url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/catalans-vs-hull/60085 | title=Super League (Sky Sports) }} On his third game back against [[Huddersfield Giants]], Tomkins suffered a hamstring injury.{{cite web | url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/sam-tomkins-suffers-major-injury-blow-during-huddersfield-giants-clash | title=Sam Tomkins suffers injury blow during Huddersfield Giants clash | date=9 August 2024 }} On 4 October, Tomkins signed an extension for the [[2025 Catalans Dragons season|2025 season]].[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2m5jn5mn2o bbc.co.uk]https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/13227781/sam-tomkins-to-play-on-in-2025-for-catalans-dragons-after-coming-out-of-retirement Two months into the season, he announced his He had scored five tries in five appearances of the season at the time of the announcement.https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/super-league-icon-sam-tomkins-announces-second-retirement-as-timeframe-confirmed Tomkins had his contract terminated two months early by mutual consent seeing him retire in August. He finished his time at Catalans as their third all time points scorer.[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cm21r9mnmk8o bbc.co.uk] == International career == ===England=== In the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup|2008 World Cup]], co-hosted by Australia and New Zealand, [[England national rugby league team|England]]'s campaign ended with a 32–22 loss to [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at the semi-final stage.{{cite news|last=Hughes|first=Ed|title=England miss out on World Cup final|url=http://www.timesonline.co.uk/tol/sport/rugby_league/article5160861.ece|access-date=30 June 2011|newspaper=The Times|date=16 November 2008|archive-date=7 November 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211107232451/https://www.thetimes.co.uk/|url-status=dead}} England responded to their exit from the tournament by replacing a number of veterans with younger players for the [[2009 Rugby League Four Nations|2009 Four Nations]].{{cite news|last=Irvine|first=Christopher|title=England look ahead as old guard makes way for youth|url=http://www.timesonline.co.uk/tol/sport/rugby_league/article6871823.ece|access-date=30 June 2011|newspaper=The Times|date=13 October 2009|archive-date=7 November 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211107232452/https://www.thetimes.co.uk/|url-status=dead}} Tomkins, who was then playing at [[Rugby league positions#Stand-off|stand-off]] for Wigan, was included in a new-look squad which featured a quarter of its players aged 21 or below. {{Quote box |quote = In broken play, he is absolutely devastating. If the defensive line is not quite strong enough, he will absolutely rip you to shreds. He is a rising star in rugby league. |source = [[Iestyn Harris]], [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] head coach, 18 October 2009.{{cite news|title=Iestyn Harris pays tribute to Sam Tomkins after England beat Wales|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/6366738/Iestyn-Harris-pays-tribute-to-Sam-Tomkins-after-England-beat-Wales.html|access-date=30 June 2011|newspaper=The Daily Telegraph|date=18 October 2009|archive-date=25 October 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091025143138/http://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/6366738/Iestyn-Harris-pays-tribute-to-Sam-Tomkins-after-England-beat-Wales.html|url-status=live}} |width = 220px |align = right |quoted = 1 }} On 18 October 2009, having played less than thirty games for Wigan, Tomkins made his international début for [[Tony Smith (rugby league b. 1967)|Tony Smith]]'s England team against [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] at [[Brewery Field]] in a warm-up match for the Four Nations tournament. The then-twenty-year-old scored a hat-trick in a 12–48 victory, with [[Iestyn Harris]], the Welsh head coach and former [[List of dual-code rugby internationals|dual-code rugby international]], praising Tomkins' contribution to the winning England team. Despite this, Tomkins was left out of the squad that faced [[France national rugby league team|France]] in the opening Four Nations fixture.{{cite news|title=Briscoe wings in for England|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_5642423,00.html|access-date=30 June 2011|newspaper=Sky Sports|date=21 October 2009|archive-date=23 October 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121023120638/http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_5642423,00.html|url-status=live}} He replaced [[Richie Myler]] for England's defeat by [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] at Wigan's [[DW Stadium]], with an impressed [[Darren Lockyer]], the Australian captain, stating after the match, "To be honest, we didn't really know their young fellas. We do now."{{cite news|last=Irvine|first=Christopher|title=Young half-back duo can galvanise England|url=http://www.timesonline.co.uk/tol/sport/rugby_league/article6916454.ece|access-date=30 June 2011|newspaper=The Times|date=14 November 2009|archive-date=7 November 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211107232452/https://www.thetimes.co.uk/|url-status=dead}}{{cite news|title=Tomkins – We were so close|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_5669582,00.html|access-date=30 June 2011|newspaper=Sky Sports|date=2 November 2009|archive-date=23 October 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121023120652/http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_5669582,00.html|url-status=live}} With Smith showing confidence in the young half-back pairing between [[Kyle Eastmond]], and Tomkins, Tomkins maintained his place in the squad which won their must-win match against New Zealand for a place in the tournament final.{{cite news|last=Irvine|first=Christopher|title=Tony Smith prepares England for the long haul|url=http://www.timesonline.co.uk/tol/sport/rugby_league/article6907226.ece|access-date=30 June 2011|newspaper=The
